forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 52. West southwest wind around 8 mph.

forecast.name: Monday
forecast.detailedForecast: Partly sunny. High near 64, with temperatures falling to around 62 in the afternoon. West wind 7 to 17 mph, with gusts as high as 25 mph.

weather_report:
In the quiet embrace of night, Downtown San Francisco rests beneath a soft shroud of clouds, wrapped in the gentle chill that comes as the city prepares for dawn. With overnight temperatures dipping into the low fifties, a light breeze from the west southwest offers a refreshing contrast to the warmth trapped within the cozy confines of homes. As day breaks, whispers of sunlight will filter through the thinning clouds, gradually revealing the iconic skyline, though one can expect the golden glow of the sun to be subdued; the marine layer will linger, particularly near the coastal fringe.

As the morning unfolds into a partly sunny Monday, expect temperatures to rise to a comfortable sixty-four degrees, then gradually slide to around sixty-two in the afternoon. Winds will dance through the streets, occasionally gusting up to twenty-five miles per hour, lending a lively spirit to the day. This mix of coastal air and urban vibrancy makes for an invigorating atmosphere; however, dressing in layers is advisable, with a light jacket or sweater that can be shed as the sun stretches its arms toward the city. The conditions invite leisurely strolls along the waterfront, where the brisk air mixes with salty breezes—an ideal backdrop for quiet reflection or a gentle chat with a friend over coffee at a sunlit café. Embrace this blend of clouds and sun, for it speaks to the very essence of San Francisco, a city continually caught between its fog-laden past and its radiant present.
